title: Do not default to a persistent dark sidebar
summary: AI-generated product apps universally include a persistent dark or glassmorphic left sidebar even when the navigation structure has only 3-4 items that would fit in a top bar.
instruction: Choose a persistent sidebar only when the navigation structure genuinely requires it. Consider top navigation, context-revealing navigation, or sliding navigation for shallow information architectures. If a sidebar is chosen, ensure it does not force a dark/light color split by reflex.
rationale: A persistent dark sidebar consumes horizontal space, forces a high-contrast dark/light split, and adds visual weight that many interfaces don't need. The sidebar is a structural choice, not a default layout frame.
violations: Left sidebar with dark background regardless of brand color scheme
Sidebar with 3-4 navigation items that would fit in a top header
Glassmorphism sidebar with readability issues on overlaid content
Sidebar takes 240-280px of horizontal space on every page
Collapse/expand hamburger toggle that reveals an equally unnecessary menu
controls: Top navigation bar for apps with shallow hierarchies (3-5 items)
Contextual sidebar that only appears when relevant to the current view
Tab-based navigation for secondary-level content categories
Light sidebar with reduced visual weight matching the main content area
borderlineCases: Desktop-first tools where sidebar is the primary command surface (Figma, VS Code)
contextualExceptions: Deep information architecture requiring persistent category navigation
detectorSignal: A wide (180-320px) persistent left-side element with navigation links, contrasted background, and a collapse toggle.
matcherProposal: Detect side-positioned nav elements that occupy >150px width, contain 3+ navigation links, and use a different background from the main content area.
detectorLimitations: Cannot assess whether the sidebar is justified by navigation depth
falsePositiveScenarios: Legitimate desktop applications that need persistent navigation
mitigationStrategy: Flag as informational with a recommendation to evaluate whether the sidebar could be replaced with a lighter navigation pattern.

fixturePlans.violation: Dark left sidebar with 4 navigation items, glassmorphism effect, and a hamburger collapse toggle.
fixturePlans.control: Light top navigation bar with the same 4 items, no sidebar.
fixturePlans.borderline: Sidebar for a developer tool with 8+ navigation sections.

cleanRoomAttestations: Developed without access to proprietary prompt collections.
